title: Opponent-Cover Pressure Features From Same-Lap Field Context
summary: F1 pit calls are often reactive: undercut, overcut, or 'box-to-overtake' against nearby rivals.
rationale: F1 pit calls are often reactive: undercut, overcut, or 'box-to-overtake' against nearby rivals. Current best solutions are mostly self-state + tyre-feasibility; they appear to underuse competitive context within the same race lap.
implementation_hint: Within each Race-Lap, compute position-neighborhood features: tyre-life rank among cars ahead/behind, fraction of top-5/top-10 on each compound, distance from field median TyreLife/LapTime_Delta by compound, and whether nearby positions just pitted on the previous lap. Feed these to a tree model or a small specialist.
expected_effect: Adds race-dynamic context that can move probabilities earlier or later around pit windows, improving discrimination among otherwise similar tyre-age states.
risk: Without true time gaps, position-based neighborhood proxies may misrepresent real undercut opportunities on some circuits.
